DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-12-2021 HEARING RE: DEBRA SCHOENBERG'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA FOR PERSONAL APPEARANCE [CODE CIV. PROC. SECS. 1985.3, 1987.1, 1987.2], THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED "EXTREMELY GOOD CAUSE" TO COMPEL MS. SCHOENBERG'S DEPOSITION. NO SANCTIONS. A REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY DETAILED FACTS (E.G. HOURS WORKED AND BILLING RATE). SUCH DETAIL IS MISSING FROM MR. PICONE'S DECLARATION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena For Personal Appearance [Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 1985.3, 1987.1, 1987.2]
Off Calendar May-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN V. PICONE III, Esq. of Hopkins & Carley (Tel: 408-286-9800) for non-party Respondent Debra Schoenberg;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEBRA SCHOENBERG's Motion To Quash Subpoena For Personal Appearance [Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 1985.3, 1987.1, 1987.2].

Counsel for non-party Respondent stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for non-party Respondent authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff has not demonstrated "extremely good cause" to compel Ms. Schoenberg's deposition.

No sanctions. A request for sanctions must be accompanied by detailed facts (e.g. hours worked and billing rate). Such detail is missing from Mr. Picone's declaration.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
